hero.headline: SSODL Online MBA in Hospital and Health Care Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Symbiosis International University's fully-online MBA with a Hospital and Health Care Management specialisation. NIRF #24 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Rs 55,000 work-experience scholarship for 2+ year professionals. Hospital Planning, Clinical Services Management, Super Speciality Hospital Administration, Healthcare Analytics, and Community Health in Sem 3-4.

sections.tldr: SSODL Online MBA Hospital and Health Care Management: Symbiosis brand (NIRF #24, NAAC A++), Rs 55K work-experience scholarship, healthcare management depth with Hospital Planning, Clinical Operations, MIS for Hospitals, Healthcare Analytics, and Quality Accreditation. Fees Rs 3.15L with scholarship, Rs 3.70L standard. Strong alternative to Amity Healthcare for Pune-region hospital management careers.

about.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Hospital and Health Care Management is offered by Symbiosis School for Online and Digital Learning, the fully-online arm of Symbiosis International University (SIU), Pune. SSODL received UGC-DEB approval in 2020-21.

SIU carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #24 University ranking (2025). Pune's healthcare sector is significant: the city hosts Columbia Asia, Jehangir Hospital, Ruby Hall Clinic, Sahyadri Hospitals, and several multispecialty private hospital chains. The SSODL Healthcare spec covers Hospital Planning, Clinical Services Management, Supportive Services Management, Super Speciality Hospital Administration, MIS for Hospitals, Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare, IT Applications for Healthcare, and Community Health.

The Healthcare Analytics subject in Sem 4 is particularly relevant for hospital management roles: few online MBA healthcare programmes include dedicated analytics for hospital performance. The Symbiosis brand recognition in Pune's healthcare sector gives SSODL graduates a meaningful advantage for hospital administration careers in western India.

sem1Subjects.name: Business Statistics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply statistical analysis to patient outcome data, hospital performance benchmarking, and healthcare quality metrics.

sem1Subjects.name: Marketing Management
sem1Subjects.description: Apply hospital marketing principles, patient acquisition strategy, and healthcare brand management for hospital management roles.

sem1Subjects.name: Financial Accounting
sem1Subjects.description: Manage hospital cost accounting, departmental budget reporting, and healthcare financial statements interpretation.

sem1Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem1Subjects.description: Apply process efficiency concepts to OT scheduling, patient flow management, and healthcare service delivery.

sem1Subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
sem1Subjects.description: Apply healthcare law, patient rights regulations, clinical negligence frameworks, and hospital licensing compliance.

sem1Subjects.name: Human Resource Management
sem1Subjects.description: Manage hospital workforce planning, clinical staff recruitment, and healthcare team performance management.

sem1Subjects.name: Research Methodology
sem1Subjects.description: Design clinical outcomes research, patient satisfaction surveys, and healthcare quality improvement studies.

sem1Subjects.name: Microeconomics
sem1Subjects.description: Apply healthcare pricing theory, insurance market structure, and demand-supply dynamics to hospital pricing decisions.

sem1Subjects.name: Technology in Business
sem1Subjects.description: Evaluate HIS, EMR systems, telehealth platforms, and healthcare technology infrastructure for hospital management.

sem1Subjects.name: Data Driven Decision Making
sem1Subjects.description: Apply hospital analytics dashboards, patient outcome data, and healthcare KPI tracking to evidence-based management decisions.

sem2Subjects.name: Organizational Behaviour
sem2Subjects.description: Manage cross-functional hospital teams, doctor-administrator relationships, and healthcare culture change.

sem2Subjects.name: Operations Research
sem2Subjects.description: Apply scheduling models, OT utilisation optimisation, and resource allocation to hospital operations management.

sem2Subjects.name: Macroeconomics
sem2Subjects.description: Interpret health sector investment trends, insurance penetration data, and healthcare policy changes for strategic planning.

sem2Subjects.name: Financial Management
sem2Subjects.description: Manage hospital capex, insurance reimbursement models, and healthcare working capital.

sem2Subjects.name: R Programming
sem2Subjects.description: Build hospital performance analytics models, patient outcome visualisations, and clinical data analysis using R.

sem2Subjects.name: Project Management
sem2Subjects.description: Apply project management to hospital expansion, new department commissioning, and accreditation preparation projects.

sem2Subjects.name: Management Information Systems
sem2Subjects.description: Evaluate HIS, PACS, and hospital management information systems for clinical and administrative data integration.

sem2Subjects.name: Business Communication
sem2Subjects.description: Write hospital board reports, clinical quality presentations, and healthcare stakeholder communications.

sem2Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour and Insights
sem2Subjects.description: Understand patient decision-making, healthcare seeking behaviour, and patient satisfaction drivers for hospital marketing.

sem2Subjects.name: Digital Marketing
sem2Subjects.description: Apply digital patient acquisition, hospital website SEO, and online reputation management for healthcare brands.

sem2Subjects.name: Design Thinking
sem2Subjects.description: Apply patient-centred design to hospital ward layouts, patient journey redesign, and healthcare service innovation.

sem2Subjects.name: Innovation Management
sem2Subjects.description: Evaluate healthcare innovation: telemedicine, AI diagnostics, remote patient monitoring, and digital health startups.

sem3Subjects.name: Project I (4 Credits)
sem3Subjects.description: Conduct a hospital management research project on quality improvement, patient satisfaction, or clinical operations.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Governance and Ethics
sem3Subjects.description: Apply healthcare governance standards, clinical ethics frameworks, and patient rights compliance to hospital management.

sem3Subjects.name: Crisis Management in Business
sem3Subjects.description: Manage healthcare crises: epidemic response, mass casualty events, and hospital reputation incident management.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Develop hospital strategic plans: capacity expansion, specialty development, and competitive positioning in local healthcare markets.

sem3Subjects.name: AI and ML for Business Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply AI diagnostics evaluation, ML-based patient triage, and predictive readmission models to hospital management decisions.

sem3Subjects.name: Fundamentals of Hospital Planning
sem3Subjects.description: Design hospital facility plans: space allocation, department adjacency planning, and healthcare infrastructure development.

sem3Subjects.name: Management of Hospital Clinical Services
sem3Subjects.description: Manage OT scheduling, ICU capacity, clinical pathway design, and clinical department performance for hospital administrators.

sem3Subjects.name: Management of Hospital Supportive Services
sem3Subjects.description: Manage hospital housekeeping, dietary, security, and biomedical equipment services as COO-track hospital management.

sem3Subjects.name: Organisation and Admin of Super Speciality Hospitals
sem3Subjects.description: Administer super speciality departments: organ transplant units, oncology centres, and cardiac surgery administration.

sem4Subjects.name: Project II (10 Credits)
sem4Subjects.description: Complete a full hospital management dissertation applying healthcare quality, operations, or finance theory to a real case.

sem4Subjects.name: Entrepreneurship
sem4Subjects.description: Build a healthcare startup pitch: diagnostics chain, digital health product, or specialty clinic network business plan.

sem4Subjects.name: Global Business Environment
sem4Subjects.description: Analyse international healthcare systems, medical tourism strategy, and cross-border hospital management models.

sem4Subjects.name: Conflict and Negotiation
sem4Subjects.description: Manage doctor-management disputes, insurance company negotiations, and vendor contract negotiations in healthcare.

sem4Subjects.name: Doing Business in India
sem4Subjects.description: Navigate NABH accreditation, Clinical Establishment Act compliance, and PMJAY empanelment for Indian hospitals.

sem4Subjects.name: Business Transformation and Organizational Turnaround
sem4Subjects.description: Lead hospital transformation: merger integration, department rationalisation, and digital health transformation management.

sem4Subjects.name: MIS for Hospitals
sem4Subjects.description: Implement hospital information systems: EMR integration, clinical data warehousing, and hospital reporting infrastructure.

sem4Subjects.name: Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare Sector
sem4Subjects.description: Prepare for NABH, JCI accreditation, and ISO 9001 certification in hospital quality management.

sem4Subjects.name: IT Applications for Healthcare
sem4Subjects.description: Evaluate telemedicine platforms, EHR systems, PACS, and healthcare IoT for hospital technology management.

sem4Subjects.name: Community Health
sem4Subjects.description: Design community health programmes, primary health centre management, and public health outreach for hospital systems.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all SSODL MBA specialisations. The Hospital and Health Care Management specialisation adds 9 subjects in Sem 3 and 10 subjects in Sem 4, with dedicated coverage of clinical services, super speciality administration, NABH accreditation, and Healthcare Analytics.

sections.whoHires:
Hospital and Health Care Management MBA graduates from SSODL Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Private hospital chains, Healthcare consulting, Insurance (health), Pharmaceutical companies, Healthcare technology startups, Public health organisations

**Entry-level roles:** Hospital Administrator, Healthcare Operations Manager, Quality Manager (NABH), Business Development Manager (hospital)

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Hospital Manager, Head of Operations (hospital), Healthcare Project Manager, COO (clinic network)

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Hospital CEO, VP Healthcare Operations, Head of Medical Affairs, Healthcare Group Director

**Top hiring organisations:** Columbia Asia Hospitals, Jehangir Hospital, Ruby Hall Clinic, Sahyadri Hospitals, Manipal Hospitals, Apollo Hospitals, Fortis Healthcare, Narayana Health, Max Healthcare, Aetna India, Niva Bupa, ICICI Lombard Health
sections.skills:
The SSODL Hospital and Health Care Management specialisation builds skills that hospital administration and healthcare management employers test at COO-track levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Hospital Planning and Clinical Services Management: facility planning, OT scheduling, clinical pathway design, and clinical department performance management for hospital administrator roles
- Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare: NABH and JCI accreditation preparation, ISO 9001 healthcare application, and quality audit management for quality head roles
- MIS for Hospitals: EMR integration, hospital information system management, and clinical data warehousing for hospital technology administration
- Healthcare Analytics (Sem 4 Business Analytics overlap): patient outcome analytics, hospital performance benchmarking, and health data analysis for evidence-based management

**Applied skills:**
- Organisation and Admin of Super Speciality Hospitals: organ transplant unit administration, oncology department management, and cardiac surgery centre operations
- Community Health: primary health centre management, public health outreach design, and community health programme administration

**Soft skills:** Managing doctor-administrator relationships, presenting hospital performance data to boards, negotiating with insurance companies, and leading hospital accreditation preparation projects.
sections.comparisons:
Comparing SSODL Online MBA Hospital and Health Care Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Hospital Healthcare Management (direct peer):** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Healthcare covers hospital management comprehensively and is a strong direct peer. SSODL adds dedicated Super Speciality Hospital Administration and Community Health subjects not typically in Amity Healthcare. SSODL carries stronger brand recognition in Pune-based private hospital chains. For all-India healthcare management careers, Amity Healthcare at a lower fee is a strong competitor. For Pune and western India hospitals, SSODL carries a meaningful brand advantage.

**vs MUJ Online MBA Healthcare (if offered):** MUJ offers a healthcare concentration within its MBA programme. SSODL Hospital and Health Care Management is a dedicated full specialisation with 9 Sem 3 and 10 Sem 4 subjects in healthcare. SSODL carries stronger specialisation depth and Symbiosis brand recognition for hospital management roles.

**vs Manipal Jaipur Online MBA (Healthcare if offered):** MUJ's healthcare focus, if available, covers clinical management broadly. SSODL's Symbiosis brand carries stronger recognition in Pune-headquartered private hospital chains and western India healthcare sector compared to MUJ in this geography.

fees.body:
The SSODL Online MBA in Hospital and Health Care Management offers 2 fee tracks based on applicant work experience:

**Track 1 — Standard (under 2 years work experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,70,000 across 4 semesters
- Semester breakdown: Rs 92,500 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 87,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,95,000 (including Rs 10,000 registration)
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,75,000

**Track 2 — With Work Experience Scholarship (2+ years experience)**:
- Application fee: Rs 1,000
- Registration fee: Rs 10,000 (one-time)
- Total program fee: Rs 3,15,000 after Rs 55,000 experience-based scholarship
- Semester breakdown: Rs 80,000 each for Sem 1-2 and Rs 72,500 each for Sem 3-4
- Year 1 total: Rs 1,70,000
- Year 2 total: Rs 1,45,000

The Rs 55,000 experience scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or opt for one-time upfront payment. Submit 2+ years work experience proof during admission to claim.

EMI is available through the university's marketing partner. This is interest-bearing EMI, not zero-cost. Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options and approval terms.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with SSODL each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and scholarship eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Symbiosis International University (SIU) and carries the SIU name with the MBA designation. The Hospital and Health Care Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from SSODL Online MBA Hospital and Health Care Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Dr. Prachi Joshi
items.liked: Management of Hospital Clinical Services and NABH Quality Accreditation subjects were directly relevant to my role at a Pune multispecialty hospital. The Symbiosis name was immediately recognised by the hospital board.
items.disliked: Super Speciality Hospital Administration could include more case studies from actual super speciality centres.
items.body: I had 5 years as a hospital administrator and the Symbiosis MBA helped me move from operations to strategic management. The Rs 55K scholarship brought fees to Rs 3.15L, which was fair. Programme quality was strong. Symbiosis brand has genuine recognition in Pune private healthcare.

items.name: Manish Sonar
items.liked: Community Health subject was unique. IT Applications for Healthcare gave me vocabulary to manage our hospital's HIS implementation.
items.disliked: Rs 3.70L is high compared to Amity Healthcare. Interest-bearing EMI is a drawback.
items.body: Good programme. Symbiosis brand works well for Nashik-Pune private hospital hiring. If you can use the work-exp scholarship and plan for the higher fee, the value proposition is reasonable.

items.name: Sunita Rao
items.liked: Strong overall structure. Healthcare Analytics gave me tools applicable to hospital dashboard design.
items.disliked: Placement support for healthcare roles was limited. Hospitals do not typically respond to online MBA job portals.
items.body: Solid credential. For Bengaluru hospital management, both Amity Healthcare and SSODL are recognised. Amity is cheaper. SSODL gives you the Symbiosis name and deeper speciality hospital coverage, but requires active self-sourced job search.

faqs.question: How does SSODL's work-experience scholarship work?
faqs.answer: Applicants with 2 or more years of documented work experience receive a Rs 55,000 scholarship off the standard Rs 3,70,000 program fee. The scholarship applies whether you pay semester-wise or in a single upfront payment. Submit proof of work experience during admission to have it applied automatically.

faqs.question: Is SSODL Online MBA the same as Symbiosis on-campus MBA?
faqs.answer: Both are awarded by Symbiosis International University. SSODL Online MBA is UGC-DEB approved and fully online. The SIU name appears on both certificates. Employers treat UGC-DEB approved online MBAs as equivalent for corporate and hospital management hiring eligibility.

faqs.question: Does SSODL offer no-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: No. SSODL EMI is interest-bearing and routed through the university's marketing partner. MUJ and NMIMS offer zero-cost EMI. Factor in EMI interest cost when comparing total programme cost.

faqs.question: Does SSODL Healthcare MBA cover NABH accreditation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Quality and Accreditation in Healthcare Sector is a dedicated Sem 4 subject covering NABH and JCI accreditation preparation, ISO 9001 application in healthcare, and quality audit management. This makes the programme directly applicable to hospital quality management roles in NABH-accredited or accreditation-seeking hospitals.

faqs.question: Is SSODL Hospital Healthcare MBA useful for doctors or only administrators?
faqs.answer: The programme is designed for hospital administrators, healthcare operations managers, and healthcare management professionals. Doctors who want to move into hospital management or healthcare leadership roles (Medical Director, COO) can also benefit, as the programme covers strategic, financial, and operational management rather than clinical subjects.

chooseIf: You want the Symbiosis brand for hospital management roles in Pune-headquartered private hospital chains and western India healthcare organisations.
You have 2+ years work experience in healthcare or hospital administration and the Rs 55,000 scholarship reduces effective fees to Rs 3.15L.
You want dedicated coverage of Super Speciality Hospital Administration, NABH Quality Accreditation, and Community Health in a single programme.
consider: If fee is the primary concern, Amity Online MBA Hospital Healthcare Management (Rs 2.25L) is a strong direct peer at a significantly lower cost and is recognised pan-India.
If zero-cost EMI matters, MUJ and NMIMS offer zero-cost EMI; SSODL does not.
If the target is Bengaluru or Mumbai hospital management and brand differentiation from Symbiosis is limited in those markets, Amity Healthcare offers comparable recognition at lower cost.
